CIVIC EDUCATION EXAMINATION — Types of Government
Class: SSS1 | Board: NERDC | Time: 40 minutes

Q1. [MULTIPLE_CHOICE] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 2]
Which of the following best defines democracy?
A) A system in which one person rules for life
B) A system in which government authority comes from the people
C) A system in which the military controls the state
D) A system in which only landowners can vote
Answer: B

Q2. [SHORT_ANSWER] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 4]
List FOUR features of a federal system of government and state ONE example of a country that operates a federal system.

Q3. [MULTIPLE_CHOICE] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 2]
The document that contains the fundamental rules governing Nigeria as a state is called:
A) The Electoral Act
B) The Code of Conduct
C) The Constitution
D) The Civil Service Rules
Answer: C

Q4. [SHORT_ANSWER] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 4]
Identify the THREE arms of government in Nigeria and name the institution that heads each arm.
   Arm 1: ________________  Headed by: ________________
   Arm 2: ________________  Headed by: ________________
   Arm 3: ________________  Headed by: ________________

Q5. [SHORT_ANSWER] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 4]
Kemi is studying for her civic education test. Help her by stating:
(a) The name of Nigeria's current constitution and the year it was adopted
(b) The number of states in Nigeria
(c) The name of Nigeria's highest court
(d) The two houses that make up the National Assembly

Q6. [TRUE/FALSE] [Bloom: REMEMBER] [Marks: 4]
State whether each sentence is TRUE or FALSE:
(a) Nigeria operates a presidential system of government. ________________
(b) The Governor is the head of the Federal Executive. ________________
(c) Universal adult suffrage means every citizen above 18 can vote. ________________
(d) A unitary system concentrates power at the central government. ________________
reference: NERDC SSS1 Civic Education exam on types of government in Nigeria covering definitions, federal structure, arms of government, and constitutional facts.
